methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Substrate is a Series A semiconductor company founded by James and Oliver Proud, currently valued at $1.0B post-money with $100M+ raised. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable set of 3 public semiconductors (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers, with particular focus on 3 same-stage private comps: Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), and Rain AI ($500M).

Two quantitative methods triangulate the valuation. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $1.0B last post-money valuation, implying $2.0B. Peer-median analysis across the 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median of $2.0B, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied without disclosed revenue; the Semiconductors sector median P/S of 24.4x across public names remains unusable for a private target. 

The triangulated valuation range is $1.7B (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $2.0B. This reflects convergence between stage-progression and peer-median methods. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion in peer valuations ($500M to $5.0B), the lack of revenue visibility to apply public multiples, and the early-stage risk profile typical of Series A semiconductor ventures.
